publicSideBand: The city replaces the public side at no cost in homeowner-initiated and annual project paths
privateSideBand: The homeowner-initiated pilot offers a $2500 reimbursement and waived permit fees while annual project blocks receive full replacement at no additional cost
fullReplacementBand: Full replacement is no direct charge when the property is directly impacted by the annual water main project and otherwise the pilot reduces but may not eliminate owner cost
housingTypeAssumption: Evanston publishes program rules and reimbursement support rather than contractor bid ranges
permitAssumption: Eligibility depends on project-area status or participation in the homeowner-initiated pilot process
restorationAssumption: City inspection scheduling permit waivers and restoration follow the city's coordinated replacement process
methodologySummary: Evanston's methodology is utility-specific because it separates annual project blocks that are fully city-funded from homeowner-initiated pilot cases that use a $2500 reimbursement plus waived permit fees.
ownerTriggerSummary: Owners pay little or nothing in annual project areas but may still face a remaining private-side contractor bill when they choose the homeowner-initiated path before the city reaches the block.
